reviews.review_description:
I’ll start with what I wish was better since I think that’s what people tend to look for in reviews. Some of the dialogue was difficult to understand. Which was a shame because Goliath is about the man’s personal experience. I wish the developers had added an option to toggle subtitles, and I do wish the microphone option was used a little more indepth.

Onto the positives: This game moved me to tears and made me want to hug my family. In this case, I consider that a positive. I won’t say too much since I don’t want to spoil the story beats, but the struggles with medication, social isolation, and trying to claw your way through a world not built for you are heavy as is. Going through that in virtual reality touches your heart, but there is light at the end of the tunnel, and the end of the game really shows that. If you have the spare money, it is genuinely worth the price.

Goliath is a storytelling experience with low physical effort. The mini games were very easy for me to understand as a new player and they fit nicely into the greater storyline. The experience is emotional and educational but interesting and immersive for the player. I really enjoyed it.

Really cool experience, the visuals and story pulled me in way more than I expected. It’s emotional, unique, and honestly kind of thought provoking. Only wish it was longer, but overall I really enjoyed it.

reviews.review_title: Loved it played it multiple times
reviews.review_description: Every tho it’s more visuals I still enjoyed every moment
I loved every moment the story was so compelling thinking about buying impulse because it looks great as well. Also I think a game about OCD would be amazing as well. Keep up the great work!

I enjoyed the story. A lot of the bad reviews are people with short attention spans, didn’t read the app description, or just don’t have an ounce of empathy to care. If you’re unlike that, you might enjoy his story. The visuals are very nice and fit the documentary nicely.
Made me cry several times. The people writing 1 stars saying that this documentary sucks are insane and are probably schizo themselves.

developer_response.body: @JarroKing13 - Thank you so much for sharing how deeply Goliath moved you---it's incredibly touching to know it resonated with you on such a personal level.

reviews.review_description: As a Schizophrenia patient this got me right in my feels and in my mind. The only thing I can say about this is THANK YOU. My voices are crying.

developer_response.body: Hi @weird.loop Thank you for your heartfelt words!

reviews.review_description: Thought it was some sort of mixed reality game but it's just a story of some guy ...

developer_response.body:
It is clearly stated in the description that Goliath is a Virtual Reality narrative experience, so we are not sure where the mixed reality aspect came from. Could you clarify?

However, your wish is our command! The next episode of the Playing with Reality collection, Impulse: Playing with Reality, is due to come out this autumn. Stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: When I load up this game, all it does is lag it goes in out in out and so forth can't exit because the lag either stops me from opening my menu or closes it before I can do anything

developer_response.body: Sorry you are having this issue, it could be something storeside that it didn’t install correctly. Please try uninstalling and reinstalling. Or you can also check you have given permission to the microphone this app will not load without granting microphone permissions.

reviews.review_description: Graphics are the pieced together Lego blocks. Interactive piece was so disappointing because it was like an afterthought. I wish it would have just been the video if they were going to integrate silly zapper shooting. The worst experiential app I've used on Oculus. Go buy Tripp or Maloka.

developer_response.body: Hi @MagicalFrijoles We are sorry you had such a negative reaction towards Goliath. Goliath is meant to be a narrative VR (clearly stated in the description) so the main idea is using VR to tell a story whereby interaction aids the storytelling, and is not the 'main course' as it would be for VR games.

reviews.review_description: This game will not load. I'm so disappointed.

developer_response.body: hi mickeymouse.721485 we are sorry you are experiencing an issue. Please try uninstalling and reinstalling the app. When prompted ensure you give the app permission to access the microphone. The app is tested and should play ok if the permissions are granted.

reviews.review_description:
I struggle with a lot of the same things.. It was literally like i was goinv through my own life from another perspective and it was the most emotional, touching, and unbelievable experience I have ever had…
This is the first time that I have literally broke down and started crying throughout a VR experience. This app is a MUST HAVE and it brought a lot of things to light for me…
Nothing interesting here also this game is for patient people and i am not one of them

When I got this game it started talking about not knowing the difference between reality and fake. It then started talking about Goliath and David and them cussing and saying stuff. If your a Christian like mean I would not recommend getting this.

developer_response.body: Hi kcskyy this documentary has no real connection to the bible's David and Goliath. The title comes from the enormous size of the protagonist who is very tall in real life, and that is the only connection to anything Biblical. The cuss words are because it's a documentary recording and overdubbing or bleeping out someone's words is bending the truth. It is a story about mental health, gaming and connection. It aims to inform people who have psychosis or schizophrenia related issues in their family. It is particularly important for teenagers who might encounter this condition, as the earlier help is found the better the outcome for the person. Interpreting what something is about correctly is important, that is why we have provided the information about this project, which you are free to read at goliathvr.io Thank you.

reviews.review_description:
I was expecting some cool things that would really showcase the quest 2’s abilities and be more entertaining. It was more than anything just a long, boring slog that I really only finished in case something epic was coming up soon. It never did. I uninstalled it immediately and regret the time I felt like I wasted. 

I appreciate the adversity Goliath has had to overcome, but we’ve all got issues, and his didn’t interest me. I’m the “illegitimate” offspring of two parents who immigrated to the US from different countries and have spent most of my life from age ten moving to another state every few years leaving behind and and all friends and family. I then grew to be a single father divorcee who has been totally screwed by family court just for being the father instead of the mother. I’ve had nothing but adversity in my life, so other people’s adversity, particularly something as mundane as mental health, is not something I’d call entertaining or compelling.

reviews.review_title: Amazing Graphics. The story was sad
reviews.review_description: The story was sad,  but so real. It was amazing to interact in this. Goliath took us into his world and how his brain sees things. One of the first games I played and I was in awe! What a beautiful way to captivate an audience.
It just feels ewwww don't recommend it
Depressing.  Losers.

It is not clear that there is no game attached to this "experience" as a psych professional I had high hopes to find a teaching tool in this, but alas it is a one person point of view experience.  It would be a way better concept for people to understand schizophrenia if there was actually a game attached to this that distorted the reality they were personally experiencing.

This was the first game I ever played with my new oculus. I was very excited. Instead of the immersion I was hoping for. I was taken on an odd experience of a self addmided mentally ill "fat" man's abridged life story. The 2 good things in the experience was the novelty of the game system and Tilda Swinton's voice acting. I wish the best for Goliath and hope he can shed the weight and worry less about what other think of him. I just feel pity for him from this game. Not fun!!!

developer_response.body: Aw thanks for the feedback we hope Jon can get back to being healthy again too and he is trying! You couldn't stretch to even two stars for the novelty game system and Tilda's acting. You are very hard to please Elysion!

reviews.review_title: I’m just starting so this is my r review
reviews.review_description: I need you to help me out with your number please
This game should not come up when you search for “kids games”

developer_response.body: This app is rated PEGI 12 with mild swearing. We have explained this in the description and we cannot control how it appears in searches.

reviews.review_description:
I got this because someone else asked me to try it and see what it was. I didn’t buy a $300 oculus to listen to some mental patient’s sob story and how he became a gamer
This game is overall by far the suckiest game i have ever played and i done think it should even be called a game at this point
Don’t waste your time on this and go do something better with your time

developer_response.body: Hi troopergordon. We are sorry you had such a negative reaction to Goliath. If you read the description of this before trying it you would have gathered it's not a game but in a new category called Experiences, a combination of storytelling and interactivity, in this case a documentary about a person's account of mental illness and how gaming helped them. Experiences about other people can be enlightening and helpful to others particularly those with family members suffering from illness.  We have tried to explain as best we can with the platform tools available that Goliath is not a game but it is about gaming.

reviews.review_description: Very bad in my opinion but it is a mint looking game would recomand if into stuning pictures
Some guy narrating his sobbing story of using drugs and making poor decisions while you’re seeing a series of non coherent animated visuals. Very boring
Very slow and depressing.   Didn't like the vulgar language.

developer_response.body: We're sorry about the language but it is a documentary and reflects real life, the recordings are from the person telling their true story. Editing it out would be tampering with the truth. The foul language warning was clearly labelled.

reviews.review_description: This doesn’t present much… the value is even less
Nice short documentary from first person narrator perspective and a few cool scenes that can only work in VR as experience,would not be the same if described
This is what you want to show someone using vr for the first time.
Life changing experience 😌 so happy to have experienced that
Just amazing experience ❤️

Visually very stunning game, but the real beauty is the story told! So happy this was free and I was able to experience it. Would definitely recommend to anybody. Any low reviews of this must be from children or people who have not experienced the struggles of life. 
Thanks for this gem!
This was absolutely amazing! Informative, beautifully narrated, and the graphics and interactive segments were so cool! It really opened my eyes!

Great visuals. Narration was perfect. The environments was good. Really immersive to the experience making me feel connected. This was a good experience to have and I’ll make sure recommend it to somebody. I loved the meaning this had and appreciate Goliath sharing his experience. All Love!
Literally the best thing I’ve ever experienced..!!
The review of seen on this game seems pretty good I’m just hoping it’s as good as the river uses
It was ok. ‘Nothing to write home about
Started my vr existence playing super hot,  zombieland, beat saber etc but this is probably the better VR experience,  certainly a great introduction into the immersive nature of the tech
I read the reviews and looked at the preview but none of it compares to actually experiencing it. It's worth your time, I promise.
